free_text: Analysis of 25Al energy levels observed in the
28Si(p,alpha)25Al reaction
Preliminary data
Experiment performed at the Oak Ridge
National Laboratory (ORNL) using Holifield Radioactive
Ion Beam Facility (HRIBF).
Natural silicon target with thickness of 50 mu-g/cm2.
Alpha particles were detected and identified in
the Silicon Detector Array (SIDAR) in an annular
telescope configuration. This array was composed of
three 300 mu-m thick dE detectors backed by three
500 mu-m thick E detectors. The array had 16
concentric strips, which covered laboratory angles
E(lab) = 18-48deg and E(lab) = 31-75deg for 40 and 42
MeV protons, respectively. for the 40 and 42 MeV
proton beams, respectively
Statistical uncertainties
Compiled by S.H.
